ocr.text: The first table presents data on various taxa with headers including extant-only taxa, combined extant and extinct taxa, 25%
incorrect extinct taxa, 50% incorrect extinct taxa, and 75% incorrect extinct taxa. The four states row shows 50 extant taxa with
values of 0.48 (0.09–0.61) for extant-only taxa, 0.49 (0.071–0.89) for combined extant and extinct taxa, 0.49 (0.14–0.85) for
25% incorrect extinct taxa, 0.49 (0.17–0.77) for 50% incorrect extinct taxa, and 0.50 (0.21–0.76) for 75% incorrect extinct taxa.
0.71), and 0.32 (0.13–0.89). The second table outlines architectural model features with headers for Stand-alone, Integrated,
Standard-based, and Service-oriented. The row for Service transferable across clinical centres lists Yes for Stand-alone, No for
Integrated, Yes for Standard-based, and Yes for Service-oriented. The Manual data-entry to CDSS minimized row has No across
Stand-alone, Integrated, Yes for Standard-based, and Yes for Service-oriented. Connected to EHR or HIS shows No for Stand-
alone, Yes for Integrated, Yes for Standard-based, and Yes for Service-oriented. The Vendor independent EHR or HIS row
includes N/A for Stand-alone, No for Integrated, Yes for Standard-based, and No for Service-oriented. The Standardized clinical
knowledge representation row contains No for Stand-alone, No for Integrated, Yes† for Standard-based, and Sometimes for
Service-oriented. Finally, the Standardized clinical data representation row shows No for Stand-alone, No for Integrated,
Sometimes for Standard-based, and Yes for Service-oriented.
